name: Index Arbitrage / ETF Arbitrage
description: Exploits temporary mispricings between an ETF and its underlying basket of securities (or index futures). When an ETF trades at a premium or discount to its Net Asset Value (NAV), buy the cheaper and sell the expensive leg. The authorized participant (AP) creation/redemption mechanism ensures convergence.
core_logic: Monitor premium_bps = (ETF_price - NAV) / NAV * 10000. BUY ETF when premium < -15bps, SELL ETF when premium > +15bps. Exit at ±3bps.

risk.main_risk: Execution risk; basket liquidity during stress

key_papers: ETF Arbitrage and Market Efficiency — various
